source-discipline.reason: Every number in the Headline Outcomes table and the Artifacts Touched table is derived from the session's own tool output (DB queries, file system scans, audit script verdict) and is replayable by re-running scripts/audit/pre_rehab_banner_audit.py against HEAD. The Cohort-vs-Handoff section explicitly names the bias direction (broader application) and the forward-reversibility of the migration. No invented metrics; no rounded-up claims.

canonical-workplan.reason: Session record names B.0 as closed and lists B.11, B.9, B.8, B.10, B.12 as remaining substantive items per the workplan structure. Phase E.1 pilot mandate (rule #10 Track 3) is named as the immediate post-Phase-B step. Workplan §B.0 self-update is named as a known-pending item; phase ordering is preserved (no Phase E content authored this session).

evidence-verification-gate-for-synthesis.reason: Banner application is the operational implementation of rule #10's final-paragraph retraction predicate. The session does NOT author any synthesis claims; the banner explicitly marks the 70 files as retracted-pending-reverification, which preserves rather than violates the synthesis gate. The 638/638 eligibility predicate is verified unchanged (rule #10 main gate).

bias_direction: Session bias toward declaring B.0 closed under a broader cohort interpretation than the handoff stated. The Cohort-vs-Handoff section is honest about the 2.3× factor and the alternative narrower-interpretation availability, but the headline framing (`B.0 closed`) presupposes the broader interpretation is correct. Owner directive 2026-05-23 confirmed the operational application stands without PI amendment (the DR is the standing reference); the narrower-cohort exit ramp remains via per-slug counter-migration. A future session that narrows the cohort would need to reopen B.0 and selectively unbanner — the language `forward-reversible` understates the work cost of doing so (~30 file edits + 1 SQL migration + 1 re-attestation per slug transitioned).
independent_reviewer_counterclaim: An outside reviewer would object that the session record claims closure on a workplan item the owner sized at ~30 BPCs while actually touching ~70. The closure claim is contingent on the DR's cohort definition, which is itself authored by the same session. The same session also added then withdrew a PI queue entry within ~30 minutes — symptomatic of insufficient up-front deliberation. Counter: the DR (DEVIATION-LOGGED, not CLEAN) and the explicit owner directive that landed mid-session make the cohort interpretation owner-overridable. The session record names this as Known Pending Work item #5 and as an Owner Action Queue item, surfacing the discretion rather than hiding it. The PI queue add/remove is part of the audit trail (visible in commit history); the human-in-the-loop pattern of 'queue, then owner directs' is the intended mechanism, not a defect.
